course_description: Revitalising Indigenous languages involves diverse processes, practices, and skills, that are underpinned by Indigenous ways of being, knowing and doing. In this course, students build critical knowledge and skills in four key topics in Indigenous Language Revitalisation; community and revitalisation networks, adult language learning, archival research and language description.

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:
SFC Suite - Indigenous Language Revitalisation: An Introduction

course_aims: This course provides students with practical and culturally-sustaining critical skills in key topics in Indigenous language revitalisation.

learning_outcomes.description: Discuss and reflect on knowledge systems, methodologies, and technologies pertinent to Indigenous language revitalisation and apply these theories in diverse contexts to reclaim Languages.
Demonstrate advanced skills in engaging with Communities, Elders, knowledge holders, and other language practitioners, enhancing networks and alliances that support language revitalisation.
Identify and evaluate culturally affirming and self-directed language learning strategies.
Articulate key theoretical approaches and concepts in linguistic description.
Locate, interrogate and appraise language and cultural material in archives and collections and contrast past and present practices and outcomes for language documentation and revitalisation.

assessment_summary.title: My Language Revitalisation Network - Presentation
assessment_summary.due_date: 2/10/2025 - 3/10/2025; Time is scheduled during the on-campus workshop for each student to give their presentation.

assessment_details.title: My Language Revitalisation Network - Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: 2/10/2025 - 3/10/2025 Time is scheduled during the on-campus workshop for each student to give their presentation.
assessment_details.task_description: In the 5-6 minute presentation, introduce yourself, your language and language network and: provide facts, statistics, data, examples or other evidence to describe your language vitality and language network. position language revitalisation in your context locally, nationally and/or internationally. draw on learnings from other language revitalisation practitioners/readings/course materials. use visuals that support your oral presentation (e.g. poster, PowerPoint, handouts). engage the audience and lead some discussion following the presentation. Statement on Generative AI & MT This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Time is scheduled during the on-campus workshop for each student to give their presentation, completing this part of the assessment.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Please be sure to apply for an extension through my.UQ ahead of the deadline if you require an extension.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: My Language Revitalisation Network - Report
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: For this report, introduce yourself, research and describe your language and language network in 1,000 words (+/- 10%) and provide facts, statistics, data, examples or other evidence to describe your language revitalisation network; position language revitalisation in context locally, nationally and/or internationally; and draw on learnings from other language revitalisation practitioners/readings/course materials. You can use the questions and headings in the 'Assessment 1 & 2 Guide and Rubrics' document in Ultra to structure your report. Statement on Generative AI & MT Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Catalogue of Language materials
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Create an inventory of materials in your language using a template Excel Spreadsheet provided by your Lecturer. The inventory will list 5 -10 resources and include details of the materials, including notes on local protocols for access and use of materials. For each item in your catalogue, include key details in at least the following fields: title format location/collection catalogue ID (if applicable) date produced or published speaker/s content type collector In addition, record your interaction with each item, evaluate its usefulness and give your reasons by recording: a record of your access (i.e. have you read or listened to the item, have you requested access, have you found about how to access this record) a star rating on a scale of 1-5 (where 5 indicates the best kind of resource) comments regarding the strengths of the resource comments regarding the drawbacks of the resource Word limit for your evaluation of each resource is 70-150 words . Statement on Generative AI & MT Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Language Learning Journal
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Longitudinal. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Keep a written journal over three weeks following the intensive workshop to document and reflect on your own language learning journey. Include descriptions of: your language learning goals language materials you have accessed one aspect of your language, using a combination of plain language and linguistic terminology language learning strategies you have tried Reflect on your above descriptions to include: new learnings about your language reflections on your engagement with people or resources in your network helpful and unhelpful strategies or ideas ideas for future language learning pathways links between theory or readings and real-life situations Word limit for your journal is 1000 words =/- 10% . Statement on Generative AI & MT Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.topic: Pre-workshop Online Learning session 1 Pre-workshop – What is Indigenous Language Revitalisation? Discuss this question in relation to the two readings.
Pre-workshop Online Learning Session 2 Models for understanding Indigenous Language Revitalisation - Language Vitality and Language Networks. Discuss readings and language vitality document.
Pre-workshop Online Learning Session 3 Language networks and resources. Discuss readings. Review assessments 1 & 2.
Pre-workshop Online Learning Session 4 Archival research and Language revitalisation. Discuss readings and plan ahead for archival research in the intensive. Orientation to assessment 3.

Intensive Day 1 Monday – Session 1 Introductions - Who is in the room? Greetings in language First Nations Languages of Australia – an overview Naming and locating languages Exploring the distribution of Australian Languages. Practical exercise – Locating patterns on the 50 Words website Monday – Session 2 Sounds and spelling English Australian Indigenous languages - distribution and state Languages in the room Describing and categorizing sounds – Place and Manner of articulation Discussion: Deciding on spelling systems, literacy, standardization and consensus Monday – Session 3 Revitalisation networks – presentation and small group mapping exercise Small group planning for presentation

Intensive Day 2 Tuesday – Session 1 Adult Language Learning – what the literature tells us Language learning strategies and tips – small group exercise Group discussion and compilation Tuesday – Session 2 Adult Language Learning and learner tools and networks Your Language Learning tools and networks – small group activity Plan for language learning - creating your toolbox and setting goals Tuesday – Session 3 Presentation preparation Visit to UQ Anthropology Museum

Intensive Day 3 Wednesday – Session 1 Ownership, authority and protocols So you think you can research! Navigating collection catalogues Wednesday – Session 2 Technology and archival research skills development Developing own language resource catalogue Wednesday – Session 3 Review activity - What do we find in a dictionary? Critical evaluation of these resources and their role in Indigenous Language revitalisation

Intensive Day 4 Thursday – Session 1 Practical skills development – Fryer Library orientation. Applying Indigenous data sovereignty principles and Indigenous Cultural and Intellectual Property Protocols. Thursday – Session 2 Practical skills development – Fryer Library, locate and view collection items Thursday – Session 3 Peer Learning - Presentations and group discussion

Intensive Day 5 Friday – Session 1 Peer learning - Presentations and Group Discussion Friday – Session 2 Peer learning – Presentations and Group Discussion Friday – Session 3 Review of assessment tasks and planning
Post-Workshop Online session 1 Debrief and follow-up. Guest talk Muruwari language activist, Suzy Chapman – My language revitalisation and learning network
Post-Workshop Online session 2 Linguistic description - making links to AUIL7201 Parts of speech in English. Linguistic terminology and method.
Post-Workshop Online session 3 Links to AUIL7201 - Language Description Orientation to intensive 2 Planning for your own Language studies and assessments
